AP.description: (Optional) The Ap-index provides a daily average level for geomagnetic activity F107, F107A, AP effects can be neglected below 80 km. If unspecified, the average of values in the 24 hours preceding the date-time  are automatically calculated from data provided by GFZ German Research Centre  for Geosciences.
AP.name: ap

F107.description: (Optional) Daily F10.7 cm radio flux for previous day (SFU). F107 and F107A values correspond to the 10.7 cm radio flux at the actual distance of Earth from Sun rather than radio flux at 1 AU. F107, F107A, AP effects can be neglected below 80 km. If unspecified, values provided by the US National Oceanic and  Atmospheric Administration are retrieved automatically.
F107.name: f107

F107A.description: (Optional) 81 day average of F10.7 flux (SFU) centered on the specified day. F107 and F107A values correspond to the 10.7 cm radio flux at the actual distance of Earth from Sun rather than radio flux at 1 AU. F107, F107A, AP effects can be neglected below 80 km. If unspecified, values provided by the US National Oceanic and  Atmospheric Administration are retrieved automatically.
F107A.name: f107a

Hour.description: UTC Hour of the day in 24 hour format
Hour.name: hour

Longitude.description: Longitude (deg) 0 to 360 deg or -180 to 180 deg
Longitude.name: longitude

info.description:
Instantly access predictive atmospheric and ionospheric models.

API requests must contain a key "API-Key" in the header (see code samples).

Visit our to obtain a key with a 14 day free trial.

Amentum Pty Ltd is not responsible nor liable for any loss or damage of any sort incurred as a result of using the API.

Copyright Amentum Pty Ltd 2024.
info.title: Atmosphere API

get.description: at a given position and time on 42-48 hour forecast horizon (5 minute resolution). NOTE: latitudes outside the interval (-90,90) are clipped to the endpoints; longitudes outside (0,360) are wrapped.

HmF2.description: Peak Height of the F2 Layer in (km).

point.description: The closest point in the dataset to the given point in request.

x-codeSamples.source:
# NOTE: Sample code will lag forecast, update this to be a datetime within 42 hours of the time of your request
curl -X GET "https://atmosphere.amentum.io/ipe/2d?latitude=21.0&longitude=-12.0&year=2024&month=3&day=25&hour=15&minute=10" -H "accept: application/json" -H "API-Key: <your_key>"

import json
import requests

url = "https://atmosphere.amentum.io/ipe/2d"

headers = {"API-Key": "<add_your_key>"}

# NOTE: Sample code time will lag forecast, update this to be a datetime within 42 hours of the time of your request
params = {
  "latitude": 21.0,
  "longitude": -12.0,
  "year": 2024,
  "month": 3,
  "day": 25,
  "hour": 15,
  "minute": 10
}

# handle exceptions
response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, params=params)
json_payload = response.json()

print(json.dumps(json_payload, indent=4, sort_keys=True))

library(httr)
library(jsonlite)

url <- "https://atmosphere.amentum.io/ipe/2d"

headers <- add_headers("API-Key" = "<your_key>")

# NOTE: Sample code time will lag forecast, update this to be a datetime within 42 hours of the time of your request
params <- list(
  latitude = 21.0,
  longitude = -12.0,
  year = 2024,
  month = 3,
  day = 25,
  hour = 15,
  minute = 10
)

response <- GET(url, query = params, headers)
json_payload <- fromJSON(content(response, "text"))

print(toJSON(json_payload, pretty = TRUE))

using HTTP, JSON

url = "https://atmosphere.amentum.io/ipe/2d"

headers = Dict("API-Key" => "<your_key>")

# NOTE: Sample code time will lag forecast, update this to be a datetime within 42 hours of the time of your request
params = Dict(
  "latitude" => 21.0,
  "longitude" => -12.0,
  "year" => 2024,
  "month" => 3,
  "day" => 25,
  "hour" => 15,
  "minute" => 10
)

response = HTTP.get(url, headers; query = params)
json_payload = JSON.parse(String(response.body))

println(JSON.print(json_payload, 4))

package main

import (
  "encoding/json"
  "fmt"
  "io/ioutil"
  "net/http"
)

func main() {
  url := "https://atmosphere.amentum.io/ipe/2d?latitude=21.0&longitude=-12.0&year=2024&month=3&day=25&hour=15&minute=10"
  
  req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", url, nil)
  req.Header.Add("API-Key", "<your_key>")
  req.Header.Add("accept", "application/json")
  
  client := &http.Client{}
  resp, _ := client.Do(req)
  defer resp.Body.Close()
  
  body, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(resp.Body)
  
  var json_payload map[string]interface{}
  json.Unmarshal(body, &json_payload)
  
  json_response, _ := json.MarshalIndent(json_payload, "", "  ")
  fmt.Println(string(json_response))
}

get.description: at a given position and time on 42-48 hour forecast horizon (10 minute resolution). NOTE: latitudes outside the interval (-90,90) are clipped to the endpoints; longitudes outside (0,360) are wrapped.

eastward_ExB_velocity.description: Easterly component of ExB drift in (m/s).

northward_ExB_velocity.description: Northerly component of ExB drift in (m/s).

point.description: The closest point in the dataset to the given point in request.

upward_ExB_velocity.description: Upward component of ExB drift in (m/s).

get.summary: Forecast 3-dimensional densities, temperatures, and velocities of charged particles in the ionosphere

tags.description:
The NOAA/SWPC Whole Atmosphere Model-Ionosphere Plasmasphere Electrodynamics (WAM-IPE) Forecast System (WFS) provides thermosphere and ionosphere forecasts on a 48 hour horizon. The model accounts for solar, space weather, and lower atmosphere forcing effects. The neutral density predictions are important for accurate orbit determinations and therefore safer space traffic management. Our wam API endpoint provides the wind components, temperature, and molecular and atomic concentrations for a given altitude, latitude and longitude, and time ahead 42-48 hours on a 10 minute grid. (The forecast horizon is 42-48 hours as the data is updated every 6 hours). NOTE: the resulting parameters are for the nearest point in the dataset (the coordinates of which are also in the response).

Visit our to obtain an API key with a 14 day free trial.
tags.name: wam

tags.description:

The model accounts for solar, space weather, and lower atmosphere forcing effects. (The forecast horizon is 42-48 hours as the data is updated every 6 hours).

Visit our to obtain an API key with a 14 day free trial.
tags.name: ipe

tags.description:
The NOAA/SWPC Whole Atmosphere Model-Ionosphere Plasmasphere Electrodynamics (WAM-IPE) Forecast System (WFS) provides thermosphere and ionosphere forecasts on a 48 hour horizon. The model accounts for solar, space weather, and lower atmosphere forcing effects. Our ipe2d API endpoint provides the Total Electron Content, Peak Electron Density, Peak Height of the F2 Layer for a given latitude and longitude, and time ahead 42-48 hours on a 5 minute grid. (The forecast horizon is 42-48 hours as the data is updated every 6 hours).

Visit our to obtain an API key with a 14 day free trial.
tags.name: ipe2d
